response.justification: Bogdan is a skilled full-stack and distributed systems engineer with strong software craftsmanship values, but he does not meet the core minimum qualifications for this role. The job requires 8 years of C++ experience — his resume lists roughly 6 months at National Instruments (Dec 2014–Jun 2015) writing C/C++ components and a bachelor thesis project in C++, with C++ absent entirely from his listed skills. The remaining stack is almost entirely TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web technologies. While he has touched distributed systems at Sociomantic and has some Kubernetes and AWS CDK exposure, there is no evidence of 5 years building large-scale cloud compute or infrastructure platforms, nor of a formal technical leadership role over cloud or DevOps engineers.
strengths: Genuine distributed systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, high-performance architecture
Strong software craftsmanship culture — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and active knowledge-sharing through workshops and talks
Broad polyglot background across many environments (web, embedded, mobile, GIS) demonstrates adaptability and systems thinking
concerns: C++ experience is effectively absent — ~6 months vs. the required 8 years; C++ does not appear in his skills section at all
No formal technical leadership tenure in a cloud compute or infrastructure domain; leadership evidence is informal (workshops, mentoring sessions)
Cloud depth is shallow — AWS CDK and Kubernetes usage appears incidental to product work rather than as a platform engineer owning compute infrastructure

key_factors.factor: Distributed systems and high-throughput work at Sociomantic
